subject: Sociology
title: Income vs Wealth
description: A quick, practical guide to Income vs Wealth.

hook.text: A doctor earning $300k with $500k in student debt is income-rich but wealth-poor. The distinction matters enormously.

buildup.text: Income is what you earn. Wealth is what you own minus what you owe. They measure different things entirely.

discovery.text: Wealth inequality dwarfs income inequality. The top 10% hold over 70% of all wealth in the United States.

twist.text: Wealth passes between generations. Income doesn't. That's why wealth gaps persist even when incomes converge.

climax.text: Policies targeting income miss the bigger story. Without addressing wealth, inequality reproduces itself endlessly.

punchline.text: Income is what you earn today. Wealth decides tomorrow.

quiz.question: Why is wealth inequality more persistent than income inequality?
options: Wealth passes between generations while income doesn't
Wealthy people always earn more income
Income and wealth are the same thing
